dependencies: Single or sole-source basis for major components, product equipment items, and raw materials, particularly in Aerospace Technologies
Key raw materials: copper, fluorspar, tungsten salts, ethylene, aluminum, molybdenum (Energy and Sustainability Solutions); nickel, steel, titanium, and other metals (Aerospace Technologies)
Majority of raw materials supply base under contract as of December 31, 2024
Dependence on supplier financial stability and access to financing
Supply chain disruptions due to global economic uncertainty, inflation, high interest rates, banking instability, geopolitical instability and regional conflicts
risks: Raw material price fluctuations and inflation
Inability of key suppliers to meet quality and delivery requirements
Supply chain disruptions and shortages of crucial components
Supplier financial difficulties or inability to scale production
Tariffs on key components resulting from trade tensions between major economies
Potential introduction of counterfeit parts into global supply chain
Supply chain constraints impacting ability to meet contractual and financial obligations

regulatory_risks: Federal, state, local, and foreign government regulations relating to environmental protection
Federal Superfund and similar state and foreign laws for hazardous waste cleanup
Climate change legislation, regulation, or international treaties
Environmental discharge regulations and remediation requirements
Asbestos-related regulations and liabilities
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act compliance
Anti-corruption laws in multiple jurisdictions
Export controls, sanctions, and trade restrictions
Import and export regulations, tariffs, sanctions, and embargoes
Data privacy and data protection laws (including GDPR and evolving U.S. state laws)
Cybersecurity laws and regulations (increasingly demanding globally)
Product certification regulations (FAA and international aviation regulatory bodies)
Government procurement regulations and compliance audits
Fire, security, safety, health care, environmental, and energy efficiency standards
PFAS legislation and regulations potentially impacting Advanced Materials business
Employment regulations and labor laws in various jurisdictions
ESG reporting requirements and evolving standards
Anti-ESG state-level initiatives in the U.S. creating regulatory uncertainty
Pillar Two Global Anti-Base Erosion Rules (15% global minimum tax)
Changes in tax laws, regulations, and judicial rulings across jurisdictions
Potential changes in U.S. federal defense spending and government contract regulations
Technology transfer and local sourcing requirements in certain foreign jurisdictions
Intellectual property protection laws varying by jurisdiction

sanction_risks: U.S. and international sanctions on Russian and Belarusian governments and entities due to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Russian government retaliatory actions against U.S. and NATO members
Export and import controls related to Russia-Ukraine conflict
Potential escalation of conflict in other regions of Europe
Increased tension between Russia and U.S./NATO members
Sanctions compliance risks across multiple jurisdictions
Trade restrictions and tariffs imposed by U.S. on goods from China and other countries
Retaliatory tariffs by China and other countries
Proposed significant tariffs by new U.S. administration (extent and focus unpredictable)
Potential for broader trade restrictions impacting imports and exports
